channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: British tourist in critical condition in Johannesburg hospital after confirmed hantavirus case on Atlantic cruise ship
item.description: The South African Department of Health has confirmed that a British national is in critical condition in a Sandton private hospital in Johannesburg after contracting hantavirus during a cruise ship vo...

item.title: Government Plans to Reopen Kenya-Somalia Border to Formalise Trade
item.description: Health CS Aden Duale has expressed support for the reopening of the Kenya-Somalia border, highlighting the potential boost to trade and revenue collection. He pointed out that while the border has bee...

item.title: NDA Leads in Puducherry Assembly Election 2026 with Key Constituency Wins
item.description: The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is all set to retain power in Puducherry for a second consecutive term following the April 9, 2026 assembly elections. Voter turnout in the Union Territory was r...

item.title: Pakistan Navy conducts humanitarian rescue of Indian-led crew after vessel malfunction in Arabian Sea
item.description: The Pakistan Navy carried out a humanitarian rescue operation in the Arabian Sea after receiving an emergency request regarding a distressed merchant vessel, MV Gautam. According to security sources, ...

item.title: Abuja Court Protest: Youths Resist SSS Arrest of Social Media Influencer Justice Crack
item.description: On Monday for Abuja, wahala burst for Federal High Court as some angry youths block State Security Service (SSS) from carrying away social media influencer, Justice Mark Chidiebere, known popularly as...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Inquiry into Criminality and Corruption Continues in Pretoria
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, which investigates criminality, political interference, and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system, continues its hearings in Pretoria. This commi...

item.title: Study Reveals How Coffee Influences Gut Health and Brain Function
item.description: Researchers at University College Cork's APC Microbiome Ireland have uncovered how coffee impacts the gut-brain axis, demonstrating that both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee can positively shape ...

item.title: N Rangasamy clinches Thattanchavady seat; NDA set to form government in Puducherry
item.description: N Rangasamy, the incumbent Chief Minister of Puducherry and leader of the All India NR Congress (AINRC), has emerged victorious in the Thattanchavady assembly constituency in the 2026 Puducherry Legis...

item.title: Tembeka Ngcukaitobi appointed as acting judge to Constitutional Court
item.description: Senior counsel Tembeka Ngcukaitobi, 49, has been appointed as an acting judge to South Africa's Constitutional Court, marking a historic moment as he is believed to be the first black advocate to be a...

item.title: Forza Capital plans $54m acquisition of Laminex site in Cheltenham for redevelopment
item.description: Fund manager Forza Capital is moving forward with plans to purchase a 5.5-hectare industrial site in Cheltenham, located in Melbourne’s south-eastern suburbs, from Fletcher Building. The site has serv...

item.title: Social Media Emerges as Leading News Source in Kenya Surpassing Traditional Media
item.description: According to the latest report from the Media Council of Kenya, social media has overtaken television and radio as the top source of news for many Kenyans. The data shows that 39 percent of news consu...

item.title: Tanzania and Kenya business leaders call for deeper economic integration to boost investment and growth
item.description: Business leaders from Tanzania and Kenya are calling for stronger economic integration between the two countries, moving beyond normal trade relations to build a unified commercial system that can att...

item.title: Ruto's Visit to Tanzania: Strengthening Ties and Addressing Parliament
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto is currently in Tanzania for a two-day state visit aimed at deepening diplomatic and economic ties between the two countries. During his trip, President Ruto will meet wi...

item.title: IHC Upholds Termination of One Constitution Avenue Lease, Buyers Can Only Claim Refund from Developer
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) on Monday confirmed the cancellation of the lease for One Constitution Avenue, a luxury residential and commercial project in Islamabad, ruling that buyers of apartments...

item.title: Australian marine biobanks aim to conserve threatened species and restore ecosystems
item.description: Across Australia, marine biobanks are emerging as vital tools to protect threatened ocean species and support ecosystem restoration. At Deakin University’s Queenscliff marine research centre, scientis...

item.title: Murang'a Governor Kang'ata Comments on His Political Plans After Leaving UDA
item.description: Murang'a governor Irungu Kang'ata has addressed recent reports suggesting he might be joining former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua’s Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP) after leaving President ...

item.title: Life in Balmain: A gentrifying peninsula that still feels like a genuine community
item.description: In this opinion piece, broadcaster and writer Chris Taylor reflects on his life in Balmain, a Sydney Harbour peninsula suburb. Although not born there, he feels a deep sense of homecoming when returni...

item.title: NDIS Funding Cuts Force Changes Across Allied Health Services
item.description: Australia's allied health sector, particularly early intervention services for autistic children, is facing a major restructuring due to significant reductions in NDIS funding. AEIOU Foundation, a lon...

item.title: Amigo Resources Identifies Nine Gold Prospect Areas in Lake Victoria Using AI Technology
item.description: A company listed on the London Stock Exchange called Amigo Resources PLC has finished an AI-assisted exploration programme in Tanzania's Lake Zone and identified nine promising areas for gold mining l...
